events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess structural welding commercial project, observed field condition: welding without qualified WPS parameters. Work through testing/inspection. Reference AWS D1.1. Need compare and produce decision fail because non-compliant and dangerous? Structural welding without qualified WPS parameters violates AWS D1.1 which requires WPS qualified and welding performed per WPS. Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. Risk high or critical? Structural welding on commercial project, unqualified WPS could lead to catastrophic failure. But observed condition is parameter non-compliance; risk high maybe critical because structural integrity. Need component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 100 for welds inspected? value irrelevant 0. confidence high 0.95. event_time etc 0. order empty. workable empty. findings: welds deposited without qualified WPS; WPS not qualified or not followed; potential lack of procedure qualification records (PQR); non-compliant with AWS D1.1 and project submittals. actions: stop work on affected welds; remove and replace or requalify WPS and re-weld; verify welder qualifications; submit WPS/PQR for approval; NDT/inspection of rework. rationale. references AWS D1.1 clauses? Need not quote mechanically but can reference. AWS D1.1 requires WPSs qualified by test or prequalified, welding performed within parameters, essential variables. Could cite AWS D1.1 Section 4 (Qualification), 4.1, 4.2, 5. WPS. Also AISC 360 maybe but reference only AWS D1.1. Let's craft JSON. Ensure valid JSON, no extra prose.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 100,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Welding was performed without a qualified Welding Procedure Specification (WPS) or outside the parameters of an approved WPS.",
    "No documented Procedure Qualification Record (PQR) or prequalified WPS basis is evident for the welds in question.",
    "Welds are non-compliant with AWS D1.1 and project submittal/inspection requirements.",
    "Structural weld integrity cannot be verified because essential variables (amperage, voltage, travel speed, preheat, filler metal, etc.) were not controlled by a qualified procedure."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop work on affected structural welding and cordon off or restrict loads on the affected connections until engineering review.",
    "Quarantine and mark all welds made under unqualified WPS parameters for 100% visual and NDT evaluation.",
    "Remove and replace welds that cannot be reliably shown to meet AWS D1.1 strength and ductility requirements.",
    "Prepare, qualify, and submit WPS/PQR packages (or use prequalified WPSs per AWS D1.1) for engineer approval before resuming welding.",
    "Re-verify welder qualifications and equipment calibration before rework.",
    "Re-inspect and document all rework welds with third-party inspection sign-off."
  ],
  "rationale": "AWS D1.1 requires structural welding to be performed under a qualified WPS with controlled essential variables. Welding without qualified WPS parameters means the weld metal properties, heat input, and procedure controls are unverified, creating an unacceptable risk to structural integrity. Because the work is through inspection and the deficiency affects code compliance and safety, the correct decision is fail.",
  "references": [
    "AWS D1.1/D1.1M, Structural Welding Code – Steel, Section 4 (Qualification)",
    "AWS D1.1, Clause 4.1 – WPS qualification required",
    "AWS D1.1, Clause 4.2 – Essential variables and limits of qualification",
    "AWS D1.1, Clause 5.3 – Production welding shall conform to qualified WPS"
  ]
}
